lever_summary: Dense i64 tensor storage. Added a LiteralBuffer::I64 { values: Arc<Vec<i64>>, lazy literals } variant mirroring the existing F64 variant, so i64 tensors carry 8 native bytes/element instead of the 24-byte Literal enum (and skip the per-element Literal::I64 match). TensorValue::new_i64_values + Value::vector_i64 now build dense i64; the same-shape i64 Add fast path folds two as_i64_slice() slices directly and emits dense i64.

alien_graveyard.primitive: dense SoA / typed columnar storage (data-model replacement)
alien_graveyard.mapping: store i64 tensors as contiguous native i64 rather than a 24-byte tagged-union Vec<Literal>; the integral analog of the F64 dense-storage work (mcqr.30 foundation)

method: Same-worker comparison on ts2: dense (vector_i64) input vs a Vec<Literal>-backed reference (eval/add_64k_i64_literal_ref) run in the same process, isolating the data-model win from cross-worker CPU variance.
command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- add_64k_i64 --save-baseline i64add

change.lever: fj-core: LiteralBufferStorage::I64 variant + from_i64_values / as_i64_slice / as_slice lazy-materialize / len / make_mut COW / Clone / owned IntoIterator arms; TensorValue::new_i64_values; Value::vector_i64 routes through it. fj-lax: eval_same_shape_i64_add gains a dense as_i64_slice fast path emitting new_i64_values, falling back to the unchanged Vec<Literal> loop.

isomorphism.ordering: unchanged: the two i64 slices are folded in the same element order as the Vec<Literal> loop
isomorphism.integer_op: unchanged: same int_op (the dispatcher's i64::wrapping_add) applied per element; wrapping at i64::MIN/MAX preserved (verified by test), no saturation/panic
isomorphism.storage_observability: as_slice() lazily materializes the identical Vec<Literal> (Literal::I64(v)) on demand, so Debug/PartialEq/serde/Deref/indexing and every existing consumer see byte-identical Literals; mutation via make_mut COW-materializes to Literals exactly like the F64 variant
isomorphism.errors: as_i64_slice() is Some only for I64 dense storage (element count matches shape); mixed/malformed declared-I64 tensors are Literal-backed -> None -> unchanged generic loop (which still returns Ok(None) on a non-I64 element, preserving fallthrough)
isomorphism.output_dtype: unchanged: dense path emits DType::I64 via new_i64_values

validation.name: fj_core_dense_i64_buffer_parity
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-core --lib dense_i64_literal_buffer
validation.result: passed: dense_i64_literal_buffer_preserves_slice_api_and_cow (slice API, materialization, serde, COW, i64::MIN/MAX)

validation.name: fj_lax_dense_i64_add_parity
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --lib dense_i64_same_shape_add

validation.name: full_lib_suites
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-core --lib && cargo test -p fj-lax --lib
validation.result: passed: fj-core 129, fj-lax 1047 (vector_i64 now dense, no regressions)

validation.name: clippy_deny_warnings
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-core -p fj-lax --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings
